danger_signals.visual_form: Flowers visibly growing out through skeletal bones, rapid physical degradation
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent consequence for souls; represents true death in the afterlife; reincarnation

danger_signals.visual_form: Demons present in the environment performing labor tasks
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural danger and unsettling presence; underworld forces at work

danger_signals.visual_form: All souls rendered as skull-faced skeletal figures
danger_signals.meaning: Death state; distinguishes deceased from living; visual reminder of mortality

danger_signals.visual_form: Pre-rendered backgrounds with 1950s retro-futuristic aesthetic, dramatic lighting and shadows
danger_signals.meaning: Danger, corruption, and moral ambiguity in the underworld setting

safety_signals.visual_form: Luxury vehicles (sports cars, ocean cruises) and the Number Nine express train
safety_signals.meaning: Reward for good deeds; direct path to eternal rest; safety and progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Travel agency structures, organized bureaucratic spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimate authority; structured path forward; order within chaos

safety_signals.visual_form: Journey through Land of the Dead toward the gate to Ninth Underworld
safety_signals.meaning: Achievable progress; souls have a defined destination and hope

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Travel agency offices, ticket counters, bureaucratic spaces
Access to luxury transport, sports cars, ocean cruises, express train
On-foot travel through underworld; often employed in land of dead; loss of faith
Performing mundane labor; vehicle maintenance; hospitality services

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Retro-futuristic 1950s aesthetic; pre-rendered static backgrounds; calaca (skeletal) population; film noir styling with dramatic shadows
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Souls pursue travel packages; employment seeking; routine death-administration bureaucracy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Express trains, luxury vehicles visible as progression markers; gate to Ninth Underworld as destination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Souls move toward final destination; increasing hope or despair based on travel package tier

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Souls permitted to visit living world; temporal shift in accessibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Souls leave underworld temporarily; return to living world relationships

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transport quality differentiation: luxury cruises/express trains vs. foot travel
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to resources and paths forward directly tied to life morality; systemic inequality

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flowers visibly erupting from skeletal frame; rapid degradation of form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Removal from underworld; transformation into new life cycle

environmental_storytelling.example: All souls rendered as calaca (skull-faced skeleton figures) communicate their deceased status without exposition
Visible luxury vehicles vs. absence of transport for certain souls tells story of moral judgment system
Anachronistic combination of Aztec afterlife mythology with film noir and mid-century design tells story of cultural fusion and timelessness
Demons performing routine maintenance and service tasks communicate the hierarchical, bureaucratic nature of the underworld
Travel agency spaces, ticket counters, and official infrastructure tell story of systematic soul administration
Manny turning his head toward objects/NPCs communicates attention and interest organically, avoiding immersion-breaking UI

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Guides souls through underworld; collects and manipulates objects; engages in dialogue trees; investigates mysteries
Follows Manny on journey through underworld; represents player's moral status and journey progression
Seek travel packages; engage in jobs if denied good transport; display hope or despair based on circumstances
Abandon journey to Land of Eternal Rest; seek employment and settle in Land of the Dead
Perform maintenance, drink service, and bureaucratic support tasks; accepted as normal infrastructure

notes: Grim Fandango communicates world-state primarily through aesthetic fusion (Aztec mythology + 1950s retro-futuristic film noir) and hierarchical transport system. Danger is signaled through supernatural elements (demons, sprouting/reincarnation) and the visual reminder of death (calaca skeleton design). Faction identity is communicated through access to resources (travel packages, vehicle quality) rather than explicit sigils, creating a political commentary on merit-based inequality. The game lacks traditional HUD elements, instead using character behavior (head-turns, inventory animation) for world-communication. Environmental storytelling is heavy: bureaucratic spaces, transport visibility, and demonic presence all reinforce the underworld's systematic nature.
